summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the solution of x + 3 > 7 and x + 3 < 5?

First: x > 4. Second: x < 2. No number is both greater than 4 and less than 2, so there is no solution.

What is a 'compound inequality'?

A compound inequality combines two inequalities using the words 'and' (intersection) or 'or' (union).

summary_sections.title: Outliers and Resistant Measures
summary_sections.content_html:
Outliers and Resistant Measures

Bolts are rejected if they are too short (x < 4.8) or too long (x > 5.2). This is an 'or' compound inequality.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: A student needs a final exam score between 70 and 100, inclusive, to pass. Their exam equation is 2x + 30, where x is hours studied. Solve 70 ≤ 2x + 30 ≤ 100 to find the range of study hours needed.

Subtract 30: 40 ≤ 2x ≤ 70. Divide by 2: 20 ≤ x ≤ 35 hours.
